title: When Automation Creates Subitem, include parent item name in subitem name 2
excerpt: The Topic is closed, but the solution is still not there. Any ideas for a workaround since monday.com does seem to be interessted in a solution?

posts.author: prmwe (Philipp)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Topic is closed, but the solution is still not there. Any ideas for a workaround since monday.com does seem to be interessted in a solution?

When Automation Creates Subitem, include parent item name in subitem name Platform discussions
We are currently using a parent board as our main board for communications with stakeholders, and the creation of tasks. I cannot find a way to use an autopopulate field on the automation where ‘if status is THIS, then create subitem’. I want to basically name things like… Video Production {item.name} Creative {item.name} Copy {item.name} Is this possible?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Phillipp,

Thank you for flagging this. I’d be happy to raise it with our automations team internally to see if this is a recipe we are considering developing.

At this stage, I’d recommend looking into our API or third party solution to achieve this - I apologise for the set back here

I will keep you updated on any information shared by our team!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for your patience Philipp!

I’ve received insight from our product team that we aim to head in this direction and address better customisation of subitems in automations through our Workflows feature - this is currently in testing, but we are aiming to have it released by the start of Q2. Subitems support should be a key feature in this release! Stay tuned for further updates and thanks for your patience

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @prmwe!

We developed an app called subitem automations that’s can solves this case. When you add a subitem it adds the name of the parent item before the subitem’s name. Here’s how it works →

Screenshot 2024-02-14 at 11.08.181928×396 12.5 KB

We’re also working on adding a few more options like adding the item’s name at the end of the subitem’s name etc.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: LADK (Leila AIT KACI)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bianca,

I am also interested in this feature. Is it already released ? If not, do you know when it should be released ?

Thanks in advance

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julietteb - I don’t suppose you have an update on this feature at all? Looks like it was in testing and due to have been released. We’re building out a workflow at the moment, but if this won’t be released we’ll probably stop here. Thanks for your help!

posts.author: Hernan (Hernan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ryan Thompson:

n update on this. Imperative to the workflows

what’s your reason for not using third-party apps?

posts.author: RyMonday (Ryan Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml: Heya, No update other than what is above. Monday is incapable of this action natively. I ended up using the subitem automations app listed above. My reason for not using 3rd party apps? Cost. If you have to do anything at scale you will incur a monthly or annual fee. I was forced to bite the bullet and pay it. Hopefully monday implements this automations in app soon.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Our Subitems Pro Automations marketplace app offers the perfect automation for this use case! It includes recipes to prefix, suffix, or replace subitem names based on any parent field.

posts.author: dl-srfrew (Simon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I am looking for a similar solution but haven’t found anything available.
The base functionality is that when an item is created, a subitem with the same title is created.

I can’t do this natively because the “create subitem” in the custom automation page does not allow variables to be passed in.

Any solutions or fixes for improved automations? I’m surprised that there are little to no dynamic values allowed to be used in Monday automations and it would be a great addition
